data.title: McGrath RentCorp Q4 2024 Financial Results
data.description:
McGrath RentCorp, a prominent business-to-business rental company in North America, has released its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024. The company reported a notable increase in revenues and net income, alongside its 34th consecutive annual dividend increase.

Financial Highlights

For the quarter ended December 31, 2024, McGrath RentCorp reported total revenues of $243.7 million, marking a 10% increase compared to the same period in 2023. Net income from continuing operations was $38.9 million, or $1.58 per diluted share, up from $32.0 million, or $1.30 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of 2023.

For the full year, total revenues from continuing operations reached $910.9 million, a 10% increase from $831.8 million in 2023. Adjusted EBITDA for the year increased by 10% to $351.7 million. Net income from continuing operations for the year was $231.7 million, or $9.43 per diluted share, compared to $111.9 million, or $4.56 per diluted share, in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

McGrath's Mobile Modular division saw an 8% increase in rental revenues, driven by growth in both commercial and education customer bases. The division's adjusted EBITDA rose by 13% to $61.0 million. However, the Portable Storage division faced a 15% decline in rental revenues due to weak demand conditions, primarily from lower commercial construction activity. TRS-RenTelco also experienced a 9% decrease in rental revenues due to continued demand challenges.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

The company announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.485 per share for the upcoming quarter ending March 31, 2025, a 2% increase over the prior year period. This marks the 34th consecutive year of annual dividend increases. Additionally, McGrath received a $180.0 million merger termination payment from WillScot Mobile Mini and incurred $63.2 million in transaction costs during the year.

Management's Perspective

Joe Hanna, President and CEO of McGrath, expressed satisfaction with the fourth quarter results, highlighting the 10% increase in companywide revenues and 5% increase in Adjusted EBITDA. He noted the strong performance of the Mobile Modular division and acknowledged the challenges faced by the Portable Storage and TRS-RenTelco divisions. Hanna emphasized the company's focus on pricing optimization, rental fleet utilization, and value-added services.

Future Outlook

For the full year 2025, McGrath RentCorp expects total revenues to be in the range of $920 to $970 million and Adjusted EBITDA to be between $345 to $360 million. The company also plans gross rental equipment capital expenditures of $120 to $130 million.

SEC Filing: MCGRATH RENTCORP [ MGRC ] - 8-K - Feb. 19, 2025

data.title: Consensus Cloud Solutions, Inc. Reports Q4 and Full Year 2024 Results
data.description:
Consensus Cloud Solutions, Inc., a global leader in digital cloud fax technology, has released its preliminary financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. The company also provided guidance for Q1 2025 and the full year 2025.

Financial Highlights

For the fourth quarter of 2024, Consensus reported revenues of $87.0 million, a slight decrease of 0.9% compared to $87.8 million in Q4 2023. This decline was primarily due to a planned decrease in the Small Office Home Office (SoHo) business, partially offset by growth in the Corporate business. Net income for Q4 2024 increased by 8% to $18.1 million, up from $16.8 million in Q4 2023. Earnings per diluted share (EPS) rose to $0.92 from $0.87 in the same period last year.

For the full year 2024, revenues decreased by 3% to $350.4 million compared to $362.6 million in 2023. Net income for the year increased to $89.4 million from $77.3 million in 2023, driven by reductions in digital marketing and interest expenses. Full-year EPS increased to $4.62 from $3.94 in 2023.

Business and Operational Highlights

Consensus saw a planned revenue reduction in its SoHo business but managed to offset this with growth in its Corporate business. The company also achieved record net cash provided by operating activities and free cash flow, alongside a significant reduction in outstanding debt.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Consensus continued its debt repurchase program, reducing its outstanding debt by $20.1 million in Q4 2024, bringing the cumulative total to $206.9 million. The company also repurchased $0.3 million worth of common stock during the quarter. Additionally, Consensus invested $7.98 million in property and equipment in Q4 2024.

Management's Perspective

Scott Turicchi, CEO of Consensus, expressed satisfaction with the company's performance, highlighting the achievement of exceeding revenue targets, maintaining strong margins, and generating record cash flows. He also noted the successful reduction of expenses in the SoHo channel and the growth in the Corporate channel's revenue.

Future Outlook

For Q1 2025, Consensus expects revenues to range between $85.0 million and $89.0 million, with adjusted EBITDA between $44.8 million and $47.8 million. Adjusted earnings per diluted share are projected to be between $1.26 and $1.36. For the full year 2025, the company anticipates revenues between $343 million and $357 million, adjusted EBITDA between $179 million and $190 million, and adjusted earnings per diluted share between $5.03 and $5.42.

SEC Filing: Consensus Cloud Solutions, Inc. [ CCSI ] - 8-K - Feb. 19, 2025

data.title: UnitedHealth says a hospital error led to Ackman's criticism of the insurer
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group said a mistake at the hospital was behind the phone call that led to a surgeon being pulled from the operating room to defend a patient's inpatient stay, a move that was eventually criticized by billionaire Bill Ackman.

UnitedHealth said that it had already approved the treatment and overnight stay for the surgeon Elisabeth Potter's patient before the call. Its representative called the hospital to discuss a request for a separate inpatient stay.

"Our representative asked to speak to the nurse caring for the patient. He did not ask to be transferred to the operating room department, did not ask to speak with the physician while she was in surgery and never asked the doctor to leave surgery," the company said.

The hospital where the incident took place was identified as HCA St. David's Medical Center in Austin, Texas, according to Bloomberg News, which first reported on the matter.

Earlier this month, Potter posted on the social media platform X, saying she was called out of an operating room to justify her patient's care to the insurer.

On February 17, Pershing Square's CEO Ackman said that UnitedHealth sent a

Bill Ackman
@BillAckman

I promised to come back to @X after I investigated the facts concerning @EPotterMD's video post about @UHC and its health insurance subsidiary, UnitedHealthcare.

To review, I made an @X post in response to Dr. Potter's videos and X posts about an overzealous representative of… https://t.co/nS2JtHPvqN

Feb 17, 2025
letter to Potter and added that he offered to pay her legal bills.

Ackman had previously posted on X that he would take a short position in the healthcare conglomerate, in a now-deleted post.

"If I still shorted stocks, I would short UnitedHealth," Ackman said in his post on February 17, adding that UnitedHealth's "profitability is massively overstated due to its denial of medically necessary procedures".

Shares of the health insurer were up 2% in afternoon trade.
